name: synthetic edge-case rule-coverage fixtures
synthetic_notice: SYNTHETIC DATA — NOT REAL JOB ADS. These records are hand-crafted to exercise specific engine rule paths and boundary conditions. They are NOT real job postings, do NOT have real source URLs, and MUST NOT be included in real-seed evaluation metrics. Report these metrics separately as 'rule harness' results. See docs/EVALUATION_PLAN.md.
collection_notes: Each record targets a specific deterministic rule path in pipeline/classifier/engine.py or pipeline/classifier/phrase_scanner.py.
source_url is null for all records — these are invented descriptions, not excerpts from real ads.
The 'name' field on each record describes the rule being exercised.
These records are suitable for checking that the engine does not regress on specific phrase patterns.
Do not use these counts in any public README claim about real-world performance.

label_definitions.worth_applying: No detected blocker for the selected visa situation, but the user should still verify with the employer.
label_definitions.verify_first: Potentially viable, but material information is missing or ambiguous.
label_definitions.likely_blocked: Strong public evidence suggests the user should not spend time applying for this selected visa situation.
label_definitions.unknown: The available public evidence is too incomplete or contradictory to judge.

records.name: contradictory-wording: no_sponsorship + sponsorship_positive in same description
records.title: Marketing Executive
records.description_text: Visa sponsorship is available for qualified candidates. However, we cannot provide visa sponsorship for this position.
records.human_notes: Contradictory wording should always produce verify_first regardless of visa situation.

records.name: future-sponsorship-phrasing: now-or-in-the-future question with no positive signal
records.title: Data Analyst
records.description_text: Will you now or in the future require visa sponsorship to work in the UK?

evidence_phrases.text: Will you now or in the future require visa sponsorship

records.human_notes: A future-sponsorship question alone (no sponsor-positive signal, no sponsor match) should produce verify_first for Graduate Route. For NSS it is also verify_first since no sponsor-register evidence is present.

records.name: future-sponsorship-phrasing: now-or-future + confirmed sponsor match + sponsorship_positive
records.title: Software Engineer
records.description_text: We are a registered visa sponsor and consider sponsorship on a case by case basis, though we cannot offer it for every role. Please indicate if you have a temporary right to work in the UK and might need sponsorship in the future.

evidence_phrases.text: consider sponsorship on a case by case basis
cannot offer it for every role
temporary right to work in the UK and might need sponsorship in the future

records.human_notes: Graduate Route: future_sponsorship_risk + sponsorship_positive + confirmed sponsor match overrides the future question to worth_applying. NSS: still verify_first because salary is absent and role-level sponsorship is not confirmed.

records.name: security-clearance: SC clearance required with no sponsorship-related phrases
records.title: Security Analyst
records.description_text: Candidates must hold current SC clearance or be willing to undergo SC clearance vetting.
records.human_notes: Security clearance is not a citizenship mandate but may require extended UK residency. Engine should produce verify_first not likely_blocked for both visa situations.

records.name: no-sponsorship-explicit-now-and-future: decisive block for graduate_route and NSS
records.title: Graduate Engineer
records.description_text: We are unable to sponsor employees, either now or in the future, so candidates must be eligible to work in the UK.

evidence_phrases.text: unable to sponsor employees, either now or in the future

records.human_notes: Explicit 'now or in the future' no-sponsorship phrase is a hard block for graduate_route (future conversion impossible) and NSS. This covers the no_sponsor_unable_employees_future_001 rule.

records.name: no-sponsorship-only: block for NSS, verify_first for graduate_route needing future sponsorship
records.title: Customer Success Manager
records.description_text: Please confirm that you have the right to work in the UK without sponsorship.

evidence_phrases.text: right to work in the UK without sponsorship

records.human_notes: Generic 'without sponsorship' phrasing: NSS is likely_blocked because sponsorship is unavailable. Graduate Route user has current RTW so likely can apply now, but future sponsorship is blocked — verify_first.

records.name: citizenship-required: decisive blocker for both visa situations
records.title: Security Officer
records.description_text: Applicants must be British citizens or UK Nationals only and able to obtain security clearance.
records.human_notes: British citizenship is a decisive blocker for all visa situations including Graduate Route.

records.name: permanent-rtw-phrase: verify_first for graduate_route, likely_blocked for NSS
records.title: Legal Associate
records.description_text: All applicants must have the right to work in the UK without restriction.

evidence_phrases.text: right to work in the UK without restriction

records.human_notes: This covers the key rule-tuning fix: permanent_right_to_work is not a hard block for Graduate Route (they have legal RTW but it is restricted). NSS is likely_blocked because they would need sponsorship from day one.

records.name: salary-missing-but-sponsor-match: NSS verify_first, GR worth_applying
records.title: Graduate Analyst
records.description_text: We are a registered visa sponsor. We can sponsor suitable candidates for this role.
records.human_notes: Sponsor match + sponsorship_positive but no salary. GR worth_applying because salary is not a gating requirement for current RTW use. NSS verify_first because salary must be checked against Skilled Worker thresholds.

records.name: no-employer: both situations unknown
records.title: Graduate Developer
records.description_text: Interesting graduate role with competitive salary.
records.human_notes: Missing employer name makes sponsor-register matching impossible. Engine should return unknown for both visa situations.

records.name: five-years-uk-residency: security_clearance verify_first for all situations
records.title: Defence Analyst
records.description_text: Candidates must have 5 years UK residency for clearance purposes.
records.human_notes: Five-year UK residency requirement is security-clearance-related, not citizenship. Both situations should verify the exact clearance requirements.

records.name: must-not-require-sponsorship-now-or-future: both likely_blocked
records.title: Operations Manager
records.description_text: Candidates must not require visa sponsorship now or in the future.

evidence_phrases.text: must not require visa sponsorship now or in the future

records.human_notes: Explicit 'now or in the future' must-not-require phrase. For Graduate Route: future sponsorship is categorically blocked so likely_blocked is appropriate. Covers no_sponsor_now_future_001 rule.

records.name: sponsorship-case-by-case-no-sponsor-match: GR worth_applying, NSS verify_first
records.title: Business Analyst
records.description_text: We consider sponsorship on a case by case basis for the right candidates.

evidence_phrases.text: consider sponsorship on a case by case basis

records.human_notes: Sponsorship-positive wording without a sponsor-register match. GR worth_applying because no blockers detected and user has current RTW. NSS verify_first because no confirmed register match and role-level sponsorship is not confirmed.
